---
title: "Authors"
subtitle: "Who wrote for the Royal Society, and when"
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

DERIV = Path("data/derivatives")
prod = pd.read_csv(DERIV / "author_productivity.csv")
top_years = pd.read_csv(DERIV / "author_year_counts_top50.csv")
collab = pd.read_csv(DERIV / "collab_size_distribution.csv")

ACCENT = "#1f77b4"
MUTED = "#a8b0bd"

n_authors = len(prod)
n_articles_with_an_author = int(prod["n_articles"].sum())
```

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
**Names are surface forms.** Author counts group by the exact name string in
the source metadata. Two spellings of the same person (e.g. *Newton, Isaac*
and *Sir Isaac Newton*) are counted separately. **1,643 articles (19%)** have
no listed author.
:::

The catalogue records **`{python} n_authors`** distinct author names across
**`{python} f"{n_articles_with_an_author:,}"`** author-article links.

## The most prolific authors

```{python}
#| label: fig-top-authors
top = prod.head(50).sort_values("n_articles")
fig = go.Figure(
    go.Bar(
        x=top["n_articles"],
        y=top["author_name"],
        orientation="h",
        marker_color=ACCENT,
        hovertemplate="<b>%{y}</b><br>%{x} articles<extra></extra>",
    )
)
fig.update_layout(
    height=900,
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=10, b=40),
    xaxis_title="Articles",
    yaxis_title=None,
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig
```

## Career span vs. publication count

Each dot is an author. Hover for the name. The horizontal axis is the number
of years between their first and last article in the catalogue (career
*span*, not active years); the vertical axis is their total article count.

```{python}
#| label: fig-span-vs-count
scatter_df = prod.dropna(subset=["span_years"]).copy()
fig = px.scatter(
    scatter_df,
    x="span_years",
    y="n_articles",
    hover_name="author_name",
    hover_data={"first_year": True, "last_year": True, "n_topics": True, "span_years": False},
    opacity=0.6,
)
fig.update_traces(marker=dict(size=7, color=ACCENT, line=dict(width=0)))
fig.update_layout(
    height=520,
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=10, b=40),
    xaxis_title="Career span (years)",
    yaxis_title="Articles",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig
```

## Timelines of the 30 most prolific authors

One row per author; one marker per year in which they published. Marker size
encodes how many articles in that year.

```{python}
#| label: fig-author-timelines
top30 = prod.head(30)["author_name"].tolist()
tl = top_years[top_years["author_name"].isin(top30)].copy()
tl["author_name"] = pd.Categorical(tl["author_name"], categories=list(reversed(top30)), ordered=True)
tl = tl.sort_values(["author_name", "year"])
fig = go.Figure(
    go.Scatter(
        x=tl["year"],
        y=tl["author_name"],
        mode="markers",
        marker=dict(
            size=4 + tl["count"] * 2.5,
            color=ACCENT,
            opacity=0.75,
            line=dict(width=0),
        ),
        hovertemplate="<b>%{y}</b><br>%{x}: %{customdata[0]} article(s)<extra></extra>",
        customdata=tl[["count"]].values,
    )
)
fig.update_layout(
    height=720,
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=10, b=40),
    xaxis_title="Year",
    yaxis_title=None,
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig
```

## How many authors per article?

```{python}
#| label: fig-collab
fig = go.Figure(
    go.Bar(
        x=collab["n_authors"].astype(str),
        y=collab["n_articles"],
        marker_color=ACCENT,
        text=collab["n_articles"],
        textposition="outside",
        hovertemplate="%{x} authors → %{y:,} articles<extra></extra>",
    )
)
fig.update_layout(
    height=400,
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=20, b=40),
    xaxis_title="Authors per article",
    yaxis_title="Articles",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(type="category")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig
```

Single-author work dominates this period, with co-authored pieces remaining
unusual until the late 19th century.
